behavior_in_task_description.reason: All tested specifics — file paths, struct name 'SegmentToLoad', field names, phase comment strings, parameter names 'is_initial_load'/'best_effort'/'is_startup', 'main_priority->check(cache_state_guard.lock())', 'std::vector<SegmentToLoad> segments', 'segments.push_back', '++failed_to_fit', Allman brace style — are explicitly stated in instruction.md.

no_solution_leakage.reason: instruction.md specifies exact implementation details: the struct name 'SegmentToLoad' with exact field names, exact phase comment strings, exact variable names 'failed_to_fit' with exact increment syntax '++failed_to_fit', exact vector declaration 'std::vector<SegmentToLoad> segments', exact method call 'main_priority->check(cache_state_guard.lock())'. This is essentially dictating the solution rather than describing the problem.

solution_uniqueness_guard.reason: Tests assert on gold-specific implementation details: 'struct SegmentToLoad' (exact name), exact phase comment strings like 'Phase 1: scan and parse all segment files', 'std::vector<SegmentToLoad> segments' (exact declaration), 'segments.push_back', '++failed_to_fit', 'main_priority->check(cache_state_guard.lock())'. An alternative correct fix using different struct names, different comments, or different batching approaches would fail these tests.

tests_verify_behavior_not_text.reason: Every test is purely text-matching: reading file contents and checking for string presence (e.g., 'assert "struct SegmentToLoad" in content', 'assert "Phase 1: scan and parse all segment files" in content'). No test compiles, executes, or tests runtime behavior of the code.

test_not_tautological.reason: A stub that simply inserts the required strings as comments into the files would pass all f2p tests. For example, adding '// struct SegmentToLoad { offset, size, kind, cache_it }' and the phase comments as dead code would satisfy the text-matching assertions without actually implementing any fix.

behavior_in_tests.reason: All behaviors required by instruction.md are tested: three-phase comments, SegmentToLoad struct, parameter renaming across all listed files, main_priority->check call, vector usage, failed_to_fit counter, and Allman style check.

informative_test_structure.reason: Tests are well-named (e.g., test_best_effort_renamed_in_interface, test_three_phase_loading_structure), have docstrings indicating pass_to_pass vs fail_to_pass, and are logically grouped by feature area.

structured_data_schema.reason: Task involves C++ code changes, not structured data output.

no_typos.reason: File paths, variable names, and commands in instruction.md match those referenced in tests without typos.

instruction_no_hint_leakage.reason: instruction.md names exact files (FileCache.cpp, IFileCachePriority.h, etc.), exact struct names, exact variable names, exact comment strings, and exact method calls. This goes far beyond describing the bug — it prescribes the exact solution code.

config_edit_scope_bounded.reason: This is a code-only task, not an agent config edit task.
